reasoning: The Lean signature assumes `[Infinite X]` (corresponding to "X is an infinite set") and concludes `IsConnected (Set.univ : Set (CofiniteTopology X))`. In Mathlib, `CofiniteTopology X` is the type synonym carrying exactly the finite complement topology (open sets are ∅ or have finite complement), and `IsConnected (univ : Set α)` is equivalent to `ConnectedSpace α` by `connectedSpace_iff_univ`. Mathlib's `IsConnected` requires the set to be nonempty plus preconnected, which matches the standard definition of a connected space; `Infinite X` provides the nonemptiness automatically. The hypothesis, conclusion, types, and quantifier structure all match the informal claim, so the formalization is strictly faithful.
